threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think you can find street parking easily on a Saturday. The locals know it’s like hunting for gold in these parts.
Caught a night bus to Brixton, and a guy tried to sell a broken phone as 'vintage'. Only in London do you find street hawkers at 3 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I heard kids arguing about who gets to play on the broken swings at Körnerpark. It’s a small chaos that feels so right.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a club with a petting zoo next door. Dance with a goat or two after a night out, it's a real vibe!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a real local by how they navigate Muni—no hesitation at transfers. Tourists still clutching their maps like a lifeline.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy juggle on the 38 Geary while a dog snoozed at his feet. Only in the Tenderloin do the absurd and the mundane ride together.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is all about the squares, but many locals prefer the quiet side streets where the real charm and hidden cafes are.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival when they’re still taking photos of the street signs. Locals just know to ignore them.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still say 'the tube' instead of 'the Overground' when you're heading to Dalston. It’s a dead giveaway.
Caught the N35 last night; a bloke was blaring Chopin from his phone, completely zoned out while the rest of us squeezed past his bag. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Watching a group of kids turn a vacant lot into a makeshift soccer field. Just pure joy and creativity.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find an open-air cinema screening retro films in an abandoned tram depot. Only in Berlin, right?

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell who's local by how they can dodge a Muni bus while juggling a burrito and still manage to spit out the latest food truck gossip.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo on the 14 leaving the club at 2 AM. He was explaining Muni routes to a tourist like it was his second job. Classic SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all bohemian cafés, but many locals head to the back streets for family-run bodegas with the best tapas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're new by the way they wander into the 'Carniceria' looking for beef instead of the jamón. A telltale sign for sure.
